title: Linked Worktrees and Parallel Branch Operations · Repository Checkpoint
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for linked worktrees and parallel branch operations and requires the smallest safe multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.
Add branch, detached, orphan, and relative-path worktrees; then lock, unlock, repair, and move them with path and device constraints.
Reason about checked-out branch exclusivity, shared refs, concurrent fetch or maintenance, and safe cross-worktree branch deletion and rewrite.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Worktree Common Perworktree evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Worktree Common Perworktree assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G53 is a quiz in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Worktree Common Perworktree has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Worktree Common Perworktree precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G53 is a quiz in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Each linked worktree contains an independent clone and object database.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Parallel Operation Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G60 is a quiz in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.
